title: Cistanthe calycina
intro: Cistanthe calycina, also known as the Calcareous Thrift, is a succulent plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white flowers and thick, fleshy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cistanthe calycina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cistanthe calycina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
